rankedGaps.label: Variables & Data Types
rankedGaps.why: Mastering variables is essential for understanding the foundation of programming in Java.

rankedGaps.label: Methods
rankedGaps.why: Understanding methods is crucial for organizing code into reusable blocks, a fundamental aspect of programming.

rankedGaps.label: Operators
rankedGaps.why: Operators are vital for performing arithmetic, comparison, and logical operations, basic functions in any program.

rankedGaps.label: Control Structures
rankedGaps.why: Control structures like if-else, switch, and loops are fundamental for flow control in programming.

rankedGaps.label: Classes & Objects
rankedGaps.why: Classes and objects are essential for defining custom data types and are the building blocks of object-oriented programming.

rankedGaps.label: Inheritance
rankedGaps.why: Inheritance allows for the creation of hierarchies of related classes, a key concept in object-oriented programming.

rankedGaps.label: Polymorphism
rankedGaps.why: Understanding polymorphism is important for using methods with different implementations, enhancing code flexibility.

rankedGaps.label: Exceptions
rankedGaps.why: Handling exceptions is crucial for managing runtime errors and ensuring robust program execution.

rankedGaps.label: Collections Framework
rankedGaps.why: The collections framework provides efficient data structures for storage and retrieval, a common requirement in programming.

rankedGaps.label: Encapsulation
rankedGaps.why: Encapsulation is vital for hiding implementation details and exposing interfaces, a principle of good object-oriented design.

rankedGaps.label: Multithreading
rankedGaps.why: Multithreading allows for concurrent execution, improving program performance and responsiveness.

rankedGaps.label: Input/Output Streams
rankedGaps.why: Understanding input/output streams is necessary for reading and writing data to files and networks.

rankedGaps.label: Serialization
rankedGaps.why: Serialization is important for converting objects to and from byte streams, useful in data exchange and persistence.

rankedGaps.label: Reflection
rankedGaps.why: Reflection allows for inspecting and modifying classes at runtime, a powerful feature for dynamic programming.

rankedGaps.label: Annotations
rankedGaps.why: Annotations provide metadata for compiler instructions and runtime checks, supporting aspects like dependency injection and validation.

rankedGaps.label: JDBC
rankedGaps.why: JDBC is essential for interacting with databases using SQL and the JDBC API, a common requirement in data-driven applications.

rankedGaps.label: Generics
rankedGaps.why: Generics enable the creation of reusable and type-safe code, improving code quality and reducing bugs.
